context.env: Real Postgres: DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/cdp (migrations applied). turbo test task is cache:false. Docker is available for LocalStack. For UI: build a runnable local app + local API adapter so Playwright can drive it.
invariants: Tenant isolation: every tenant-scoped row carries workspace_id NOT NULL; every query filters by workspace_id. Service-role Lambdas bypass RLS so they MUST scope by workspace_id in code; the admin app uses the user JWT so RLS applies.

workspace_id is NEVER from a client payload — it comes from the API-key/authorizer context or a trusted server-set field. The frontend sends the active-workspace claim via its JWT; the API resolves scope from the authorizer, never from a body field.

Role enforcement (§3A): owner/marketer/accounting are workspace-scoped; system-admin is the only cross-tenant role and every cross-tenant access is audit-logged. The UI shows only what the role permits; the API enforces it regardless of the UI.

All sends (broadcasts §9A and automation actions §9B) go through the SAME outbox -> Dispatcher pipeline (§9) and pass suppression -> frequency-cap -> quiet-hours; sending is gated on workspace active/verified.

Idempotency: broadcasts dedupe per (broadcast_id, profile_id); the automation runner must tolerate retries without double-advancing (optimistic checks / updated_at); image uploads + metering rollups are idempotent.

LOCAL-only: AWS (SES/SNS/S3/SQS) are MOCKED (aws-sdk-client-mock) or run on LocalStack; never hit real AWS. Do NOT mock Postgres in the integration tier — use the real local DB on localhost:5433. The editor must emit MJML, never hand-rolled email HTML.

Logic in pure injected functions; Lambda handlers stay thin. Reuse the prior harness (packages/db testutil: adminPool, hasDatabaseUrl, applyMigrations; runPlanInWorkspaceTx; SqlStatement {text,values}; @cdp/segments resolveAudience; @cdp/email; the dispatcher core).

Determinism: turbo test task is cache:false — keep it. Unique workspace UUIDs + file-local namespaces per integration file (events.event_id / ses_message_id are GLOBAL keys). No cross-file races. Frontend e2e (Playwright) must be deterministic (seeded data, stable selectors).
Scope discipline: implement exactly what the cited sections require for THIS phase — no features beyond the spec. Keep the cost model EXACTLY as §20 (direct usage + even split of fixed costs).
context.loop: This phase has a FRONTEND/UI surface. The widest quality loop is a REAL BROWSER: build the UI with Vite + Preact (or React) and verify with Playwright e2e against a locally-running app + local API (thin adapter/serverless-offline over the Lambda handlers + LocalStack). Use the core GrapesJS + MJML plugin (BSD-3, free) — never the paid Studio SDK; the editor must emit MJML. Seed deterministic data and use stable selectors so the e2e is reliable.

criteria: Login (Supabase Auth) + a workspace switcher: a user in two workspaces sees only the active workspace; switching re-scopes all reads/writes (no cross-bleed). Verified in a real browser (Playwright).

Role-aware UI (§3A): marketer cannot see/use user/domain/billing admin; accounting sees billing but cannot edit segments/automations; owner can do both; system-admin sees the cross-company console. Verified in-browser per role.

The core screens work end-to-end against the local API: build a segment (dynamic + manual), compose+send a broadcast, build a automation, view dashboards/suppressions/profiles — exercising the real phase 2–11 backends.
The API enforces role + workspace scope regardless of the UI (a forbidden action is rejected server-side, not just hidden).
scope: A Vite + Preact (or React) SPA in /web wired to the API Gateway REST API (via the Lambda handlers; stand up a LOCAL API — e.g. a thin adapter/serverless-offline + LocalStack — so the SPA + Playwright run end-to-end locally). Supabase Auth login + a workspace switcher that sets the active workspace_id claim and re-scopes the app.

Role-aware, workspace-scoped screens (§3A capability matrix): workspace onboarding wizard (§10A), workspace settings (members+roles, sending domain status), segment builder (dynamic rule-AST + manual hand-pick/CSV) with live size preview, broadcast composer, automation/workflow visual builder (trigger/wait/condition/action/exit), email editor (GrapesJS+MJML from phase 11), dashboards (deliverability/segment sizes/send volume), suppression list, profile explorer, billing/usage view (owner+accounting), system-admin cross-company console (system-admin only).
The UI shows only what the role permits AND is scoped to the active workspace; the API still enforces roles + scope independently.
phase.specSections: §12 (Vite SPA, Supabase Auth, workspace switcher, role-aware + workspace-scoped UI, segment builder dynamic+manual, broadcast composer, automation/workflow builder, email editor, dashboards, suppression list, profile explorer, billing/usage view, system-admin console), §3A (roles), §10A (onboarding wizard UI)
phase.title: Admin frontend (role-aware SPA)

e2eFlows: login scoped to A -> only A data -> switcher B -> only B data + nav change -> create in B persists workspace_id=B
dynamic segment + live preview + manual hand-pick/CSV -> persisted
broadcast send (SES mocked) + automation graph save + enrollment; dashboards/suppression/profiles
owner onboarding wizard step (domain -> DNS records -> check mocked DNS/SES)
implUnits: 1. services/local-api scaffold (Hono, pg pool 5433, pnpm dev:api, wire playwright webServer)
2. local authorizer middleware mirroring services/authorizer + POST /auth/dev-login
3. routeTable + enforce wrapper reusing contextFromAuthorizer/enforceRoute/handleAdminAccess/writeAuditEntry [GATE AC4]
4. route handlers delegating to cores via scopedQuery [GATE scope/preview/onboarding/audit]
5. web apiClient (token-bearing, no body workspace_id) + auth/session + workspace-switch store reusing @cdp/tenancy [GATE api-client/switch unit]
6. web capability Nav + AppShell + WorkspaceSwitcher [GATE nav unit + role-visibility e2e]
7. web SegmentBuilder (AST + manual/CSV + preview) [GATE segment-ast unit + build-segment e2e]
8. web BroadcastComposer + AutomationBuilder [GATE automation-builder unit + broadcast-automation e2e]
9. web OnboardingWizard/WorkspaceSettings/Dashboards/SuppressionList/ProfileExplorer/BillingUsageView/SystemAdminConsole (lean read-mostly)
10. Playwright seed fixture (multi-workspace via adminPool, data-testid) + 4 e2e specs [GATE all e2e]

localApi.design: New pnpm package services/local-api: thin Hono http server (pnpm dev:api, drives Playwright). Does NOT reimplement logic. Per request: (1) local authorizer middleware mirrors services/authorizer (verify dev JWT, load memberships + platform_admins from real PG, authorize(), inject SAME string context shape as API GW); (2) handlers call services/api contextFromAuthorizer() then enforceRoute(ctx,capability) via routeTable — role enforced SERVER-SIDE independent of UI; (3) every DB op via @cdp/db scopedQuery (workspace_id from token never body) on real pg :5433; (4) delegates to existing cores: @cdp/segments compileWhere/validateAst/addManualMembers/resolveAudience+COUNT, services/broadcast runBroadcast (SQS+SES mocked), services/automation-runner validate/enroll, services/onboarding start/check/activate (DNS+SES mocked); (5) cross-tenant reads via handleAdminAccess+writeAuditEntry. Same server reused by integration tests (synthetic context) and e2e (full HTTP). dev-login mints tokens for seeded users.

mocks: SES aws-sdk-client-mock at dispatcher/email boundary (assert config set, never real mail)
DNS resolver + SES status for onboarding check (scripted)
SQS sender in broadcast/dispatch path (capture outbox-id)
local/mock auth dev-login minting JWT {sub,workspace_id,role,is_platform_admin} (spec-permitted for e2e)
Supabase Auth client mocked in unit
plan.notes: Local API is the load-bearing new piece: REUSES @cdp/tenancy (can/requireCapability/switchActiveWorkspace/buildJwtClaims), services/api (contextFromAuthorizer/enforceRoute/handleAdminAccess/writeAuditEntry), @cdp/db scopedQuery, and the segments/broadcast/automation/onboarding cores with the SAME injected-context shape as the real authorizer -> role+scope enforcement identical to production, proven SERVER-SIDE in integration (real PG) AND e2e (real browser). Capabilities: manage_content, manage_workspace_users, manage_sending_domain, view_billing, view_all_workspaces. Priority = §18 gates: AC4 server enforcement + AC1 switch/no-cross-bleed highest value, proven both tiers; e2e is widest loop + primary gate. SES always mocked; Postgres never mocked. skipIf(!hasDatabaseUrl). Mock/local auth spec-permitted for e2e (token carries active workspace_id+role). Dedicated-IP advisor + full metering are Phase 13 — only read-only surfaced here.
pureFunctions: routeTable {method,path}->Capability (single source for server enforcement + nav)
screens: SegmentBuilder (dynamic AST + manual hand-pick/CSV) + live size preview

assertions: switchActiveWorkspace updates active workspace_id + recomputes role
buildJwtClaims yields new workspace_id+role, prior query state invalidated
data selector keyed on active workspace returns nothing from prior workspace
wait ISO-8601/delay parseable; condition references whitelisted targets
identity ONLY from authorizer context, body workspace_id ignored
A and B overlapping external_ids; A token returns only A via scopedQuery
A requesting B-owned id -> 404/empty not B's row
writes persist workspace_id=A from token never body
switch to B reads only B, no A leak
system-admin GET /admin/workspaces returns A and B
reading B while active!=B writes admin_audit_log via handleAdminAccess
POST /segments/preview compiles via compileWhere workspace_id prepended, real COUNT scoped
owner /sending-domain/start returns DNS record set + persists sending_identity
login owner@A/marketer@B boots scoped to A lists only A segments
switch to B re-renders only B segments, A gone
role nav changes on switch (owner controls in A hidden in B)
create in B persists workspace_id=B in real PG
marketer: members/domain/billing/system-admin NOT in DOM, content IS
accounting: billing visible, content editors absent
dynamic segment rule-builder + live size preview + save -> segments row+definition in PG
manual segment hand-pick + CSV import + save -> segment_memberships source=manual scoped to workspace
broadcast pick segment+template + send -> status advanced + outbox rows (SES mocked)
automation visual builder save -> definition validates+persists, enrollment for seeded profile
dashboards/suppression/profile pages render real workspace-scoped data

instructions: Read CDP-BUILD-SPEC.md sections: §12 (Vite SPA, Supabase Auth, workspace switcher, role-aware + workspace-scoped UI, segment builder dynamic+manual, broadcast composer, automation/workflow builder, email editor, dashboards, suppression list, profile explorer, billing/usage view, system-admin console), §3A (roles), §10A (onboarding wizard UI).
FIRST write the failing tests from the plan (red), then implement to green. Logic in pure injected functions; handlers/components thin.
Honor every invariant — tenant isolation, role enforcement, idempotency, and "all sends go through the Dispatcher guards" are pass/fail.

Mock AWS (aws-sdk-client-mock) or use LocalStack; integration tier uses the REAL local Postgres (never mock the DB). For UI phases, verify with Playwright in a real browser against the locally-running app + local API; seed deterministic data.

Run the relevant `pnpm test` (with DATABASE_URL), plus Playwright e2e for UI phases, `pnpm typecheck`, `pnpm lint`. Iterate until GREEN including a COLD turbo run. Confirm no regression in earlier phases. Report actual command output.
Implement ONLY what the cited sections require. Keep the §20 cost model exact. The editor must emit MJML, not hand-rolled HTML.
prompt.outputFormat: JSON: { filesCreated: string[], filesModified: string[], testsPass: boolean, testSummary: string, e2eSummary: string, typecheckOk: boolean, lintOk: boolean, commandsRun: string[], notes: string }
prompt.role: senior full-stack TypeScript engineer building a serverless multi-tenant CDP + Preact SPA, test-first
prompt.task: Implement "Admin frontend (role-aware SPA)" to satisfy its test plan and §18 criteria. Red -> green -> refactor.

Role-aware, workspace-scoped screens (§3A capability matrix): workspace onboarding wizard (§10A), workspace settings (members+roles, sending domain status), segment builder (dynamic rule-AST + manual hand-pick/CSV) with live size preview, broadcast composer, automation/workflow visual builder (trigger/wait/condition/action/exit), email editor (GrapesJS+MJML from phase 11), dashboards (deliverability/segment sizes/send volume), suppression list, profile explorer, billing/usage view (owner+accounting), system-admin cross-company console (system-admin only).
